title: AI-Driven Development — Run Claude Code in Agent Pods | SkyDeck.ai
description: Run Claude Code and OpenClaw around the clock in persistent Agent Pods. The main terminal survives disconnects, /workspace and ~/.claude persist, and spend stays governed.
lede: Run Claude Code and OpenClaw in persistent Agent Pods that keep working after you disconnect — pair on one agent at a time, with spend guardrails on every run.
html:
Run Claude Code and OpenClaw around the clock — in persistent pods your team can share and your admins can govern.

Give your coding agents a place that keeps running

AI-driven development stops being a series of throwaway chats when your agent has a home. An Agent Pod is a persistent cloud workspace: launch it from GenStudio, choose Claude Code, OpenClaw, or a custom image, and start work in a real terminal. The main terminal survives your disconnects, so a long refactor or a full test run keeps going after you close the laptop. Idle pods auto-sleep to save spend and resume exactly where they left off, so an overnight job is waiting for you in the morning.

  • Kick off a long Claude Code session and let it work while you step away.
  • Run OpenClaw as a detached gateway that other tools on the pod can call.
  • Preview the app in a built-in browser pane on the pod's exposed ports, right beside the terminal.
  • Give the whole team one governed workspace instead of a dozen scattered local setups.

Your context carries over

Nothing about a pod is disposable. Everything in /workspace persists across sessions — your repo, your build, your dependencies — and Claude Code pods also keep ~/.claude, so credentials, history, and settings come back when the pod resumes. Wake it a week later and the agent picks up where it stopped, with no re-setup. The main terminal is the session that outlives your connection; extra tabs get a short grace window before they release, so a dropped signal never costs you your place.

Pair on an agent without stepping on each other

Two engineers on one Claude Code session usually means collisions. On SkyDeck, exactly one Driver holds the keyboard while everyone else observes live and read-only. Request or give control in a click to hand the session to a reviewer, a teammate, or the next time zone. That makes real code review, onboarding, and follow-the-sun debugging happen inside the same terminal instead of over a screen-share.

  • One Driver at a time — no two people typing into the same agent at once.
  • Read-only observers — reviewers and new hires watch the real session, not a recording.
  • Follow-the-sun — pass control across time zones to keep an agent moving on a long task.

GenStudio for everything around the code

Not every task needs a terminal. GenStudio gives every engineer a workspace to reach any model for the work around the code: talk through an architecture, turn a ticket into a plan, review a diff, or draft the docs. It is the same chat your engineers already reach for, with the team's shared prompts a click away and every conversation attached to the project it belongs to.

  • Any model, one interface — switch between OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google without leaving the workspace or losing your context, with no vendor lock-in.
  • Shared prompts and tools — publish the ones that work so every engineer starts from the same baseline.
  • Scheduled automation — let a routine run checks or summaries on a cadence and deliver the results to Slack.

Ship fast, stay inside the budget

Around-the-clock agents are only safe if spend is bounded. The Control Center governs every pod: set a monthly budget and choose to block, warn, or allow overage; cap spend per member and per group; limit how many pods run at once; and auto-sleep idle ones. Admins decide who reaches which models and agents through SSO, DLP, and RBAC, and watch every running pod — its driver, its agent, its cost — from one screen. Start with the Agent Pods guide, or see how it fits together on the Agent Pods page.

Get started
